title: Weak OTP or Missing MFA Step for OT VPN

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 1.5.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 1.5 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.14: Weak OTP or Missing MFA Step for OT VPN.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.14' running on pan:globalprotect or pan:auth, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-7d
| eval mfa=coalesce(factor_type, auth_method, mfa_mechanism)
| where stage="login" AND (isnull(mfa) OR mfa="none" OR mfa="password-only")
| lookup ot_vpn_profiles.csv portal OUTPUT requires_mfa
| where requires_mfa="true"
| stats count by user, portal, public_ip, host
| sort - count
description: Identifies VPN authentications that skip second factor or reuse legacy token methods for paths into OT jump zones.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes vpn and sourcetypes pan:globalprotect. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Map portals to MFA policy; (2) upgrade GP app versions enforcing MFA; (3) block legacy profiles; (4) weekly exception report to IAM. Indexes required: `index=vpn`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:globalprotect`; Lookups: `ot_vpn_profiles`, `ot_vpn_profiles.csv`.
visualization: Table, Single value (violations), Time chart. Key fields: `host`, `mfa`, `portal`, `public_ip`, `user`.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:globalprotect` or `pan:auth`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Map portals to MFA policy; (2) upgrade GP app versions enforcing MFA; (3) block legacy profiles; (4) weekly exception report to IAM.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-7d
| eval mfa=coalesce(factor_type, auth_method, mfa_mechanism)
| where stage="login" AND (isnull(mfa) OR mfa="none" OR mfa="password-only")
| lookup ot_vpn_profiles.csv portal OUTPUT requires_mfa
| where requires_mfa="true"
| stats count by user, portal, public_ip, host
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Weak OTP or Missing MFA Step for OT VPN (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 1.5)** — Identifies VPN authentications that skip second factor or reuse legacy token methods for paths into OT jump zones.

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:globalprotect` or `pan:auth`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vpn; **sourcetype**: pan:globalprotect. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vpn, sourcetype="pan:globalprotect", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **mfa** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where stage="login" AND (isnull(mfa) OR mfa="none" OR mfa="password-only")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where requires_mfa="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, portal, public_ip, host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT firewall and the approved zone-to-zone matrix for that window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Single value (violations), Time chart.
grandmaExplanation: We spot VPN authentications that skip second factor or reuse legacy token methods for paths into OT jump zones. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
